title: 10.1 Meeting Someone

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Haú, kȟolá! Tókheškhe yaúŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Taŋyáŋ waúŋ. Níš tókheškhe yaúŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Taŋyáŋ waúŋ. Tókheš eníčiyapi he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: ___ emáčiyapi. Níš tókheš eníčiyapi he?

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: ___ emáčiyapi. Waŋčhíŋyaŋke iyómakiphi.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Míš-eyá. Tókša akhé.

english_translation: Hello, friend! How are you? — I'm doing well. And you? — Good. What's your name? — They call me ___. And yours? — They call me ___. Nice to see you. — Me too. Until next time.

title: 10.2 At Home (Parent and Child)

exchanges.speaker: Iná
exchanges.lakota: Čhiŋkší, kiktá yo!

exchanges.speaker: Čhiŋkší
exchanges.lakota: Naháŋȟčiŋ maȟwá, Iná.

exchanges.speaker: Iná
exchanges.lakota: Waná híŋhaŋni. Owáyawa ektá lá kte.

exchanges.speaker: Čhiŋkší
exchanges.lakota: Oháŋ. Maločhíŋ. Wóyute wačhíŋ.

exchanges.speaker: Iná
exchanges.lakota: Aǧúyapi na asáŋpi hé wíyeya.

english_translation: Son, wake up! — I'm still sleepy, Mom. — It's morning now. You'll go to school. — Okay. I'm hungry. I want food. — Bread and milk are ready.

title: 10.3 Asking for Help

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Ómakiya yo.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Oháŋ. Táku yačhíŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Slolwáye šni. Tókheškhe ečhúŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Ú ye. Kipázo kte.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Philámayaye.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Tókȟa šni.

english_translation: Help me. — Okay. What do you want? — I don't know. How do you do it? — Come here. I'll show you. — Thank you. — No problem.

title: 10.4 Weather Talk

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Aŋpétu wašté, inčhéye?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, éyaš maǧážu kte nachéče.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Maȟpíya kiŋ sápa áye.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Thípi ektá uŋglípi kte.

english_translation: Nice day, isn't it? — Yes, but it'll probably rain. — The clouds are getting dark. — Let's go home.

title: 10.5 Checking on Someone

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Tókheškhe yaúŋ he? Nikȟúža he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Kítaŋla makȟúža. Natá mayázaŋ.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Šika. Pȟežúta yúta yo.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Oháŋ. Tókša taŋyáŋ waúŋ kte.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Asníkiya yo.

english_translation: How are you? Are you sick? — A little sick. My head hurts. — Poor thing. Take medicine. — Okay. I'll be fine later. — Rest up.

title: 10.6 At a Gathering

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Taŋyáŋ yahípi! Iyótaka po / pe.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Philáuŋyayapi. Líla wašté lél uŋhípi kiŋ.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Wóyute wačhíŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ! Maločhíŋ.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Waháŋpi na tȟaló waná wíyeya.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Wópȟila tȟáŋka!

english_translation: Welcome! Sit down. — Thank you. It's good we came. — Want food? — Yes! I'm hungry. — Soup and meat are ready. — Big thanks!

title: 10.7 Shopping for Ceremony Supplies

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Čhaŋlí na čhaŋšášá kiŋ duhá he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, lél yúha waúŋ. Tóna yačhíŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Waŋží-ží na šiná kiŋ lé nakúŋ.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Šiná tónakča he?

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Wikčémna núŋpa sáŋm záptaŋ.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Oháŋ, opȟéwatȟuŋ kte ló.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Akhé wóphila.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Héčhetu. Wóčhekiye wašté yuhá yo.

english_translation: Do you have tobacco and red willow bark? — Yes, I have them here. How many do you want? — A little of each and this blanket too. — How much is the blanket? — Twenty-five. — Okay, I'll buy it. — Thanks again. — That's right. Have a good prayer.

title: 10.8 Planning an Activity Together

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Aŋpétu kiŋ lé táku ečhúŋk'uŋpi kte he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Blé ektá uŋyáŋpi kiŋhán wašté kte.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Wašté kštó! Hokšíla kiŋ nakúŋ awíčhauŋglapi kte he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, hokȟúwa wačhíŋpi načhéče.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Tóhaŋ uŋyáŋpi kte he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Wiyótȟaŋhaŋ iyóhakam. Nakáha líla makȟáta.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Héčhetu. Wóyute na mní awáŋglapi kte.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Na owíŋža čík'ala waŋží nakúŋ.

english_translation: What should we do today? — It would be nice if we go to the lake. — Great! Should we bring the boys too? — Yes, they probably want to fish. — When should we go? — After noon. Right now it's really hot. — That's right. We'll bring food and water. — And a small blanket too.

title: 10.9 Discussing Someone's Health

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Uŋčí tókhel uŋ he? Líla awáčhaŋmi.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Kitáŋla yazáŋ uŋ kštó. Čhaŋkpéhola kiŋ yazáŋ.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Šíka. Pȟežúta thípi ektá yá he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, htálehaŋ yá. Pȟežúta k'úpi.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Wašté. Táku awágli owákihi he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Wahánpi na aǧúyapi awágli kiŋhán iyókiphi kte.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Oháŋ, htȟayétu ektá wahí kte ló.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Philámayaye. Líla iyókiphi kte kštó.

english_translation: How is Grandmother doing? I'm really thinking about her. — She's a little sick. Her knee hurts. — That's too bad. Did she go to the hospital? — Yes, she went yesterday. They gave her medicine. — Good. Can I bring her anything? — If you bring soup and bread she'll be happy. — Okay, I'll come by this evening. — Thank you. She'll be very happy.

title: 10.10 Teaching a Traditional Skill

exchanges.speaker: Uŋčí
exchanges.lakota: Čhuŋwé, lé kȟáŋ omáyakihi kte he?

exchanges.speaker: Čhuŋwé
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, Uŋčí! Táku kȟáŋpi kte he?

exchanges.speaker: Uŋčí
exchanges.lakota: Haŋpá wakáǧiŋ kte. Nipȟehíŋ awáčhi kiŋhán lé ečháŋuŋ kte.

exchanges.speaker: Čhuŋwé
exchanges.lakota: Wašté kštó! Tóhakam tókhel omáyakiya yo.

exchanges.speaker: Uŋčí
exchanges.lakota: Itókaheyaŋ, tȟahá kiŋ čhókata baksápi. Uŋyúza yo...heháŋ.

exchanges.speaker: Čhuŋwé
exchanges.lakota: Heháŋ?

exchanges.speaker: Uŋčí
exchanges.lakota: Héčhel waŋží iyúha yutítaŋ uŋyúza na heháŋ wikáŋ kiŋ apáȟtapi.

exchanges.speaker: Čhuŋwé
exchanges.lakota: Uŋčí, líla waúŋspekȟiya wašté kštó.

exchanges.speaker: Uŋčí
exchanges.lakota: Iná mitȟáwa kiŋ héčhel omákhiya. Na iyéš húŋku. Héčhel héhaŋyaŋ uŋkáǧapi s'a.

english_translation: Granddaughter, can you help me with this? — Yes, Grandmother! What are we going to make? — I'll make moccasins. If you want to sew I'll do this. — Good! Show me how later. — First, we cut the hide in the middle. Hold it...like that. — Then? — Like that we hold each one tight and then stitch the thread. — Grandmother, you're a really good teacher. — My mother taught me like that. And her mother before her. That's how we've always made them.

title: 10.11 Returning Home After a Long Absence

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Háu, kȟolá. Líla tȟéhaŋ waŋblák šni ló.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háu! Tókiya yaúŋ he? Líla awáčhičhiŋ waúŋ ló.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Otȟúŋwahe tȟáŋka waŋ él wówaši waúŋ. Waŋná waglí ló.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Wašté ló! Líla uŋkíničiyuškiŋpe. Thíyata taŋyáŋ yahí.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, makȟóče kiŋ lé líla iyómakiphi. Otȟúŋwahe tȟáŋka kiŋ iyómečhilačhe šni.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Héčhetu. Lél mitȟáoyate kiŋ iyúha uŋpí ló. Aŋpétu kiŋ lé wóphila.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Akhé lél uŋyáŋkiŋ kte ló. Tȟuŋkášila philámaya.

english_translation: Hello, friend. I haven't seen you in a long time. — Hello! Where have you been? I was thinking about you a lot. — I was working in a big city. Now I've come home. — Great! We're really glad to see you. Welcome home. — Yes, I love this land. I didn't like the big city. — That's right. Here all our people are together. Today is a day of thanks. — I'll be staying here again. Thank you, Grandfather.

title: 16.1 Visiting Relatives

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Háu kȟolá! Taŋyáŋ yahí ló. Tímahel ú yo.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háu! Philámayaye. Tokíyataŋhaŋ wahí na líla tȟehíŋ waúŋ.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Čhuwíta ló. Pȟežúta sápa yačhíŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ! Líla wašté kte ló.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Thawíču yaúŋ he? Uŋčí kiŋ waŋyáka wačhíŋ.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, čhíŋkš kičhí okówažila iyótakapi. Tókša hí kte ló.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Wašté. Wahéphi wahpáŋpi na wahánpi yúha yaúŋ.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Wóphila tȟáŋka! Uŋčí tókhel uŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Taŋyáŋ uŋ. Anpétu wašté-wašté yuhá uŋ ló.

english_translation: Hello friend! Welcome. Come inside. — Hello! Thank you. I came from far and was traveling a long time. — It's cold. Want coffee? — Yes! That would be great. — Is your wife with you? Grandmother wants to see her. — Yes, she and the kids are sitting in the car. She'll come soon. — Good. We have tea and soup. — Big thanks! How is Grandmother? — She's doing well. She's having good days.

title: 16.2 At the Store (Mazóphiye ektá)

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Tókhel! Táku yačhíŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Aǧúyapi na asáŋpi na wičháyažipa čhaŋháŋpi wačhíŋ.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Aǧúyapi kiŋ lél na asáŋpi kiŋ ká.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Lé tónakča he?

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Záptaŋ na záptaŋ — wikčémna.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Wašté. Na čhaŋháŋpi kiŋ lé nakúŋ.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Oháŋ. Akhé wahíyaŋ pe.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, philámayaye kštó!

english_translation: Hello! What do you want? — I want bread and milk and honey. — The bread is here and the milk is over there. — How much is this? — Five and five — ten. — Good. And this sugar too. — Okay. Come again! — Yes, thank you!

title: 16.3 Talking About the Weather

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Aŋpétu kiŋ lé líla tȟaté ló. Wíyažu waŋží hí kte načhéče.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, maȟpíya sápa kiŋ héna kȟíya hí ló.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Maǧážu kiŋhán thíyata uŋyáŋkiŋ kte ló.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Čhuwíta nakúŋ. Ptaŋyétu kiŋ wanáȟčiŋ hí kte.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Wanípȟala kiŋhán wá kiŋ líla óta hí kte načhéče.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Héčhetu ló. Čhaŋ kiŋ pahí uŋkókihipi kiŋhán wašté kte.

english_translation: Today is really windy. A storm is probably coming. — Yes, those dark clouds are coming this way. — If it rains, we'll stay at home. — It's cold too. Autumn is coming soon. — If winter comes, there will probably be a lot of snow. — That's right. If we can gather wood, that will be good.

title: 16.4 At an Elder's Home

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Uŋčí, tókhel yaúŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: Uŋčí
exchanges.lakota: Á, taŋyáŋ waúŋ kštó. Nitȟáŋkaš kičhí yahí he?

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, thimáhel škaŋ uŋ.

exchanges.speaker: Uŋčí
exchanges.lakota: Wašté kštó. Takúku yúta wačhíŋpi kiŋhán wahánpi yuháŋ.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Philámayaye, Uŋčí. Tóhakam wóyažu awaŋíčhiyaŋke kte.

exchanges.speaker: Uŋčí
exchanges.lakota: Nitȟáŋkaš Lakȟótiyapi oyák'uŋ čha líla iyómakiphi kštó.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Uŋkíyapi ló. Líla él éwačhaŋmi.

exchanges.speaker: Uŋčí
exchanges.lakota: Hó, héčhetu. Wakȟáŋyaŋ úŋ ye.

english_translation: Grandmother, how are you? — Ah, I'm doing well. Did you come with your grandchild? — Yes, she's playing inside. — Good. If they want to eat something, I have soup. — Thank you, Grandmother. Later I'll come check your medicine. — I'm so happy your grandchild is learning Lakota. — We're trying. I think about it a lot. — Good, that's right. Live in a sacred way.

title: 16.5 Encouraging a Child to Speak Lakota

exchanges.speaker: Até
exchanges.lakota: Čhiŋkší, Lakȟótiya éya yo.

exchanges.speaker: Čhiŋkší
exchanges.lakota: Tókhel ehé kte he?

exchanges.speaker: Até
exchanges.lakota: "Philámayaye" — héčhel oyáka yo.

exchanges.speaker: Čhiŋkší
exchanges.lakota: Philámayaye!

exchanges.speaker: Até
exchanges.lakota: Wašté ló! Waŋná, "Aŋpétu wašté" eyá yo.

exchanges.speaker: Čhiŋkší
exchanges.lakota: Aŋpétu wašté!

exchanges.speaker: Até
exchanges.lakota: Héčhetu ló! Kȟoktá waŋží: "Tókhel yaúŋ he?" — tokíl la he?

exchanges.speaker: Čhiŋkší
exchanges.lakota: Tókhel yaúŋ he?

exchanges.speaker: Até
exchanges.lakota: Taŋyáŋ waúŋ! Čhiŋkší, líla owáyaspahe ló. Wakȟáŋyaŋ Lakȟótiya iyáya yo.

exchanges.speaker: Čhiŋkší
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, Até! Akhé uŋspémakiya yo!

english_translation: Son, say it in Lakota. — How should I say it? — "Thank you" — say it like that. — Thank you! — Good! Now, say "Nice day." — Nice day! — That's right! One more: "How are you?" — where is it going? — How are you? — I'm doing well! Son, you learn fast. Speak Lakota in a sacred way. — Yes, Dad! Teach me again!

title: 16.6 After a Ceremony (Wóčhekiye Iyóhakam)

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Wóphila Tȟáŋka. Líla wašté ló.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ. Wóčhekiye kiŋ líla wasúta ló.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Tȟuŋkášila nawíčhaȟ'uŋ ló.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Héčhetu. Thiwáhe kiŋ iyúha él uŋpípi na wašté.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Wóyute waŋná wíyeya he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, wiŋyáŋ kiŋ waháŋpi na tȟaló na aǧúyapi kȟaȟéyapi.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Hókahé! Uŋyútapi kte ló.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Mitákuye Oyás'iŋ.

english_translation: Big thanks. It was really good. — Yes. The prayer was very strong. — Grandfather heard them. — That's right. All the families were there together and it was good. — Is the food ready now? — Yes, the women prepared soup and meat and bread. — Let's go! Let's eat. — All My Relations.

title: 16.7 Extended Dialogue: Storytelling Night

exchanges.speaker: Tȟuŋkášila
exchanges.lakota: Ú po, ú po. Aŋpétu kiŋ lé ohúŋkakaŋ waŋ oníčiyakapi kte ló.

exchanges.speaker: Thakóža 1
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, Tȟuŋkášila! Táku oyáke kte he?

exchanges.speaker: Tȟuŋkášila
exchanges.lakota: Iktómi otȟúŋkakaŋ waŋ oyáke kte.

exchanges.speaker: Thakóža 2
exchanges.lakota: Iktómi! Hé líla wamáyaŋk waŋ hokšíla šíča yélo!

exchanges.speaker: Tȟuŋkášila
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, Iktómi líla ksápa éyaš líla witȟkó nakúŋ. Hé kiŋ wauŋspéuŋkȟiyapi s'a. Naȟóŋ po...

exchanges.speaker: Thakóža 1
exchanges.lakota: Naúŋȟ'uŋpi ló!

exchanges.speaker: Tȟuŋkášila
exchanges.lakota: Wašté. Ehánni, ehánni, líla ehánni — Iktómi čhaŋkú waŋ él ománi. Líla okhíŋni na líla lokhíŋ. Uŋkáŋ...

exchanges.speaker: Thakóža 2
exchanges.lakota: Uŋkáŋ?

exchanges.speaker: Tȟuŋkášila
exchanges.lakota: Uŋkáŋ kȟaŋǧí waŋ čhaŋ akáŋl yaŋké. Iktómi wablák na ȟeyá: "Kȟolá! Wóyute duhá he?"

exchanges.speaker: Thakóža 1
exchanges.lakota: Kȟaŋǧí kiŋ iyáyupte he?

exchanges.speaker: Tȟuŋkášila
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ. Kȟaŋǧí kiŋ ȟeyá: "Wóyute wahá éyaš niyé čha ničúŋ kte šni." Na kiŋyáŋ iyáya!

exchanges.speaker: Thakóža 2
exchanges.lakota: Iktómi héčhel ečhúŋpi s'a ló!

exchanges.speaker: Tȟuŋkášila
exchanges.lakota: Héčhetu ló. Iktómi héčhel ečhúŋpi s'a — éyaš uŋspéuŋkȟiyapi. Wičháša waŋ wamánuŋ čhíŋ kiŋhán tȟáŋiŋ kte s'a. Héčhel ohúŋkakaŋ kiŋ uŋspéuŋkȟiyapi ló.

english_translation: Come, come. Today I'll tell you a story. — Yes, Grandfather! What will you tell? — I'll tell an Iktómi story. — Iktómi! He's a really tricky bad boy! — Yes, Iktómi is very clever but also very foolish. He teaches us. Listen... — We're listening! — Good. Long ago, very long ago — Iktómi was walking on a road. He was very tired and very hungry. And then... — And then? — And then a crow was sitting in a tree. Iktómi saw him and said: "Friend! Do you have food?" — Did the crow answer? — Yes. The crow said: "I have food but I won't give it to you." And he flew away! — Iktómi always does that! — That's right. Iktómi always does that — but he teaches us. When a man wants to steal, it will become obvious. That's how the stories teach us.

title: 16.8 Extended Dialogue: At a Powwow

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Háu! Líla wówačhi tȟáŋka ló aŋpétu kiŋ lé.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ! Wačhípi kiŋ líla wašté kštó.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Čhiŋkší wačhí he?

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, wačhí čhíŋ! Šiná lúta kiŋ kíč'uŋ na wanáȟčiŋ oíyokiphi.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Wašté ló. Líla walówaŋpi wašté škhé.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Háŋ, walówaŋpi kiŋ tokíyataŋhaŋ hípi he?

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Pȟahá Sápa etáŋhaŋ hípi načhéče.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Á, héčhetu. Na hokšíla kiŋ škaŋ wačhíŋpi.

exchanges.speaker: A
exchanges.lakota: Oháŋ. Tókša wóyute kiŋ wíyeya kiŋháŋ uŋyútapi kte ló.

exchanges.speaker: B
exchanges.lakota: Aǧúyapi skúyela na wahánpi wačhíŋ kštó!

english_translation: Hello! There's a really big dance today. — Yes! The dancing is very good. — Is your child dancing? — Yes, she wants to dance! She's wearing her red shawl and she's happy. — Good. I hear the singers are really good. — Yes, where did the singers come from? — They probably came from the Black Hills. — Ah, that's right. And the boys want to play. — Okay. Later when the food is ready we'll eat. — I want fry bread and soup!
